categories.general: Generic improvements to various aspects of shinobi-hood, useful for most characters.
categories.skill: Revolves around a specific skill (or set of skills). Used to gain mastery in a specific skill or new abilities revolving around it.
categories.chakra: Improves the efficiency of chakra usage and grants access to new natures of chakra molding (elemental, medical, etc.).
categories.ninjutsu: General improvements to Ninjutsu plus specific improvements for jutsu types — Clone, Elemental, Medical, Fuinjutsu, etc.
categories.genjutsu: General improvements to Genjutsu plus specialized improvements to Genjutsu lines or keywords.
categories.taijutsu: Improves Taijutsu generally; specific advancements to Fighting Stances and unarmed combat.
categories.bukijutsu: Bukijutsu-specific weapon-group training feats (Bullseye, Lance, Reaper, Raid, Ronin, etc.). Source PDF marks these explicitly 'Category: Bukijutsu' rather than Taijutsu.
categories.critical: Maximize the potential of critical hits — increase frequency or apply additional effects.
categories.clan: Improve a specific clan's abilities (or acquire a different clan's abilities via Bloodline, Latent). Found in Tsunade's Studies Clan Compendium.
categories.class: Non-subclass-specific improvements to a class. Found in Orochimaru's Observations Class Compendium.
categories.archetype: Partial access to a different class's abilities (similar to multiclassing). Some grant entirely new abilities based on foreign groups. Only takeable through Downtime.

actionEconomy.attack: Replaces one of the attacks granted by the Attack action.
actionEconomy.free: Free Action — used when the source text simply says the character can do the thing without specifying a time cost.
actionEconomy.special: Takes longer than a single turn (one minute, several minutes, hours, or downtime). See the entry for the exact duration.
actionEconomy.action-or-reaction: Either an Action or a Reaction at the player's discretion.
actionEconomy.full-turn: Costs your entire turn (movement + action + bonus action).

sourceKind.none: Standard feat — available to any character meeting the prerequisite.
sourceKind.class: Granted by / restricted to a specific class. The 'source' field names the class.
sourceKind.subclass: Granted by / restricted to a specific subclass. The 'source' field is 'ClassName.SubclassName'.
sourceKind.clan: Granted by / restricted to a specific clan. The 'source' field names the clan.
sourceKind.feat: Reserved for cross-feat references (currently unused on feats themselves).

tags.rare: Optional inclusion at the DM's discretion. Not automatically assumed available at every table; potentially stronger than baseline feats.
tags.repeatable: Can be taken more than once, often selecting a different option each time.

abilityScoreIncrease._note: Per-feat field of the same name is an array containing every ability score the player may choose from for the feat's +1 bump. An empty array means the feat grants no ability-score improvement. Multi-choice feats list every eligible option so a 'show me feats that can bump Strength' filter is a single contains check.

feats.name: Action Surge
feats.description:
You can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. You gain the following benefit:
• Once per turn, on your turn, you can take one additional action. Once you use this feature you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.
• Beginning at level 17, you can gain an additional usage of this feat per long rest.

actions.name: Action Surge
actions.description: Take one additional action on your turn. Once per long rest (twice from level 17).

feats.name: Advanced Study
feats.description:
You are an advanced learner and capable student. You generally excel at grasping concepts far above your skill set with amazing potential.
• Increase one ability score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You learn an additional jutsu that is 1 rank above what your Highest Rank Jutsu Known is according to your Class table. This does not count against your jutsu known.
• The next time you would hit 4th, 8th, 12th and 16th levels, you learn one additional jutsu of 1 rank higher than your Highest Rank Jutsu Known jutsu rank.

feats.name: Agile Feint
feats.description:
You know how to use your agility to grant you advantage and your opponent's disadvantage. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• Reduce the Bulk of all Light Armors by 1.
• You can use the Feint skill action with Dexterity (Acrobatics) and without Mastery in Deception. You may perform Feint before making an attack, as part of the same action.
• When a creature misses you with a melee attack you can use your reaction to make an attack of opportunity against the triggering creature.

actions.name: Feint (Agile)
actions.description: Make a Feint skill action using Dexterity (Acrobatics) — no Mastery in Deception required — as part of the same action used to make an attack.

actions.name: Reactive Strike
actions.description: When a creature misses you with a melee attack, make an attack of opportunity against them.

feats.name: Alert
feats.description:
Always on the lookout for danger, you gain the following benefits:
• You add your full proficiency bonus to your initiative, instead of half.
• You may add your Wisdom modifier in place of Dexterity to your Initiative bonus.
• You can't be surprised while you are conscious.
• You become aware of any creature casting a jutsu with Hand Seals (HS) within 60 feet of you. This does not immediately tell you the creature's location, only that Hand Seals were made with intent to cast a jutsu.
• You have advantage on checks made to find hidden creatures.
• Other creatures do not gain advantage on attack rolls against you as a result of being hidden from you.

feats.name: Athlete
feats.description:
You have undergone extensive physical training to gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Strength or Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain a Swim, climbing and wall running speed equal to your normal speed.
• Increase your maximum bulk by +5.
• Long Jump increased to 10 + Strength score and High Jump increased to 5 + Strength score.
• You have advantage on grapple checks against creatures whose Strength score is lower than yours.

feats.name: Clashing Adept
feats.prerequisite: Proficiency in either Ninshou or Martial Arts, Level 4+
feats.description:
You specialize in the art of Clashing, gaining the following benefits:
• Choose between Ninshou or Martial Arts. When you participate in a Jutsu Clash, treat your ranks of Mastery in your chosen skill as +1 higher, so long as you are proficient with the skill.
• You can always tell if a jutsu possesses the Clash keyword just from seeing it cast.
• You do not become Dazed as the result of losing a Clash.
• When you succeed a Jutsu Clash, you can choose to knock the target back 30 feet.
• Select two jutsu that you know. These become your Clashing Jutsu (changeable on a long rest). Your Clashing Jutsu gain the Clash keyword, and once per rest you may cast these jutsu as a Reaction to being targeted by a jutsu or effect with the Clash keyword, ignoring the listed casting time.

actions.name: Clash Reaction
actions.description: Once per rest, when targeted by a jutsu or effect with the Clash keyword, cast one of your two designated Clashing Jutsu as a Reaction, ignoring its normal casting time.

feats.name: Clashing Expert
feats.description:
You have mastered the art of Clashing, gaining the following benefits:
• You gain +1 ranks of Mastery in the skill selected with the Clashing Adept feat.
• While in a Jutsu Clash and Bloodied (below 50% of your maximum hit points), you gain a +1d4 bonus to your Clash checks. Additionally, for every failed Death saving throw you possess, increase your Clash Checks by +1.
• When you lose a Jutsu Clash, you do not become Concussed, and gain resistance to the damage dealt.
• When you succeed a Jutsu Clash, you can choose to knock the target prone.
• You may denote two additional jutsu you know as Clashing Jutsu. Once per long rest when you initiate a Clash with your Clashing Jutsu, you may declare this clash to be your Fated Clash. Fated Clash: you always treat your Clash as a Best of 3, regardless of the rank of the jutsu used. You may also clash with Arts, regardless of your jutsu's rank. Lastly, if the creature you are Clashing with would boost their Clash Checks with any form of dice-based bonus (such as Tenacity Die), you roll the same amount of bonus die, of the same die size, to your Clash Checks.

feats.name: Dungeon Delver
feats.description:
Alert to the hidden traps and secret doors found in many dungeons, you gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You have advantage on Perception and Investigation checks made to detect the presence of traps.
• You have advantage on saving throws made to avoid or resist traps.
• You have resistance to the damage dealt by traps.
• You can search for traps as a bonus action, instead of an Action.

actions.name: Search for Traps
actions.description: Search for traps as a bonus action instead of an Action.

feats.name: Durable
feats.description:
Hardy and resilient, you gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• When you roll a Hit Die to regain hit points, the minimum number of hit points you can regain spending hit dice equals twice your Constitution modifier (Min. 2).
• Your hit points maximum increases by an amount equal to twice your level when you gain this feat.
• Whenever you gain a level thereafter, your hit points maximum increases by an additional 2 hit points.

feats.name: Elusive Target
feats.description:
You're far too agile to ever be caught off guard or be pushed into a corner. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You may take the Hide action as a bonus action, and you may do so even when lightly obscured.
• Creatures do not gain advantage on attacks against you as a result of being Restrained or Prone.
• If you are subjected to an attack or jutsu that requires a Dexterity save, on a successful save you take no damage and suffer no effect.
• When you are forcefully pushed more than 10 feet away in any direction, if you would end your movement within 10 feet of cover large enough to hide behind, you may spend your reaction to move behind it and take the Hide action.

actions.name: Hide
actions.description: Take the Hide action as a bonus action; can hide even while only lightly obscured.

actions.name: Cover Dive
actions.description: When forcefully pushed more than 10 feet, if you'd end your movement within 10 feet of cover large enough to hide behind, move behind it and take the Hide action.

feats.name: Gourmand
feats.description:
You have mastered a variety of special recipes, allowing you to prepare exotic dishes with useful effects. You gain the following benefits:
• As a Bonus action, you can inspect a drink or plate of food within 10 feet of you and determine whether it has been altered in any positive or negative way and what it does without having to make a check.
• Over the duration of a short rest, if you prepare Special food using the Chef feat, you enhance its flavor. Creatures who gain the benefit of your special food become immune to the Envenomed condition and gain resistance to poison damage until your next rest.
• During a long rest, you can spend a use of your Cooking Kit to prepare and serve a meal that helps you and your allies recover from the rigors of adventuring. The meal serves up to six people, and each person who eats it regains 3 Hit Dice. Additionally, they increase their maximum hit points by, and gain additional hit points equal to, 4d8 for the next 8 hours.

actions.name: Inspect Food/Drink
actions.description: Inspect a drink or plate of food within 10 feet to determine whether it has been altered (positively or negatively) and what it does. No check required.

feats.name: Hand Seal Expert
feats.description:
You've practiced utilizing Ninjutsu & Genjutsu in close quarters and in contested conflicts, learning techniques that grant you the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• When making a ranged Ninjutsu or Genjutsu attack while you are within 5 feet of a hostile creature, you do not have disadvantage on the attack roll.
• When a creature would attempt to interrupt a jutsu you cast on your turn, they must attempt the ability check to interrupt regardless if they have a feature or jutsu that says otherwise. They increase the DC to interrupt your jutsu by +2.

feats.name: Helping Hand
feats.description:
Your presence in a scrap tends to elevate your comrades. You gain the following benefits:
• You can use the Help action as a bonus action.
• When you use the Help action to aid an ally in attacking a creature, increase the range of the Help action by 10 feet.
• You can help a creature with a skill check you are not proficient in.
• You can help two allies targeting the same creature within range when you use the Help action this way.

actions.name: Help
actions.description: Take the Help action as a bonus action; range increased by 10 feet for attack-aid; can help two allies targeting the same creature.

feats.name: Inspiring Leader
feats.description:
You can spend 10 minutes inspiring your companions, shoring up their resolve to fight. When you do so, choose up to six friendly creatures (which can include yourself) within 30 feet of you who can see or hear you and who can understand you.
Each creature can gain temporary hit points and temporary chakra points equal to your Proficiency bonus + your Charisma ability score. Also, each creature gains two Leader Die, which are d4s. A creature can spend one Leader Die when they make an attack roll, skill check, initiative, or saving throw, no more than once per roll.
A creature can gain the benefits of this feat no more than once per rest.

actions.name: Inspire
actions.description: Spend 10 minutes inspiring up to six friendly creatures within 30 feet who can see/hear/understand you. Each gains temp HP + temp chakra equal to your prof bonus + Charisma score, plus two Leader Die (d4) usable on an attack, check, initiative, or save (once per roll). Once per rest per creature.

feats.name: Inspiring Presence
feats.description:
Your presence on the battlefield is a source of inspiration. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• As an action, you can let out an inspiring battle cry. Select a number of creatures that can hear you equal to your proficiency bonus. Each gains a bonus to their next skill check or attack roll equal to your Charisma modifier.
• As a bonus action, you let out a reassuring howl, ending the Frightened or Charmed condition on yourself and a number of allies that can hear you equal to your proficiency bonus (Min 1).
• As a reaction, when you or a creature you can see would make a saving throw, you let loose a bracing howl. The selected creature gains proficiency in the saving throw if they were not already. This bonus lasts until the end of the current turn.
• Once you have used each of this feat's abilities once, you must complete a rest before you can use them again.

actions.name: Inspiring Battle Cry
actions.description: Select creatures that can hear you up to your proficiency bonus. Each gains a bonus to their next skill check or attack roll equal to your Charisma modifier. Once per rest.

actions.name: Reassuring Howl
actions.description: End the Frightened or Charmed condition on yourself and a number of allies that can hear you (up to your proficiency bonus, min 1). Once per rest.

actions.name: Bracing Howl
actions.description: When you or a creature you can see would make a saving throw, the selected creature gains proficiency in that save (if not already), until the end of the current turn. Once per rest.

feats.name: Maneuverable
feats.description:
You've learned that you are at your best when you're on the move. You gain the following benefits:
• Your speed increases by 10 feet.
• When you would make a Dexterity saving throw on your turn, you gain a +2 bonus to the save.
• Once per turn, when you move at least 10 feet, you gain a 1d4 bonus to your next attack roll before the end of the current turn.
• When you make a melee attack against a creature, you don't provoke opportunity attacks from that creature for the rest of the turn, whether you hit or not.

feats.name: Master Weaver
feats.description:
You have practiced casting Ninjutsu or Genjutsu in the midst of combat, learning techniques that grant you the following benefits:
• You have advantage on Constitution checks that you make to maintain concentration on jutsu when you are maintaining two or more of them at the same time.
• Select one jutsu you know of C-Rank or lower that you must concentrate on. When you cast this jutsu at its base rank, you do not need to spend chakra to maintain concentration at the beginning of each of your turns. You can switch this jutsu when you complete a long rest.
• When a hostile creature's movement provokes an opportunity attack from you, you can use your reaction to cast a Ninjutsu or Genjutsu at the creature, rather than making an opportunity attack. The jutsu must have a casting time of 1 action, must require a Ninjutsu or Genjutsu attack, and must target only that creature.

actions.name: Reactive Jutsu Strike
actions.description: When a hostile creature's movement provokes an opportunity attack from you, cast a Ninjutsu or Genjutsu at them instead. The jutsu must have a 1-action cast, require a Ninjutsu/Genjutsu attack, and target only that creature.

feats.name: Mobile
feats.description:
You are exceptionally speedy and agile. You gain the following benefits:
• Your speed increases by 10 feet.
• You can Dash as a bonus action.
• Your movement speed cannot be reduced below half as a result of a jutsu or trait. Your movement speed can still be affected as a result of a condition such as Restrained or Grappled.
• You gain advantage on saving throws and checks to resist the Grappled or Restrained conditions.
• You ignore naturally occurring difficult terrain.

actions.name: Dash
actions.description: Take the Dash action as a bonus action.

feats.name: Monkey's Grip
feats.description:
Your make-do skills and superb chakra molding let you use everyday objects in unforeseen ways. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Strength score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You are proficient with improvised weapons, and attacks with them count as Chakra-enhanced for the purpose of overcoming resistances and immunities.
• Once per turn, when you attack with an improvised weapon, you may add a bonus equal to your Intelligence modifier to the damage roll.
• You may replace a Bukijutsu's weapon component with any item your DM considers a close-enough replacement.

feats.name: Quick Witted
feats.description:
Great ideas come to you naturally, often when your life depends on it. You always have a plan, or at least parts of it. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You can use your Intelligence modifier instead of your Dexterity modifier when making Initiative checks.
• When you would make a Dexterity saving throw, you can instead make an Intelligence saving throw. You can use this effect twice per long rest.

feats.name: Resilient
feats.description:
Choose one ability score. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase the chosen ability score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in saving throws using the chosen ability score.

feats.name: Seal Weaver
feats.description:
You've practiced casting jutsu in quick succession, learning techniques that grant you the following benefits:
• When you cast a Ninjutsu or Genjutsu with a casting time of 1 action, you may use your bonus action to cast a jutsu of the same type with the same casting time.
• When you cast a Ninjutsu or Genjutsu with a casting time of a bonus action, you can reduce the cost by half as long as it is no higher level than your highest rank known.
• Once you've used either effect of this feat twice, you must complete a long rest before you can use it again.

actions.name: Quickened Cast
actions.description: After casting a Ninjutsu or Genjutsu with a 1-action cast, use your bonus action to cast another jutsu of the same type with the same casting time. Twice per long rest combined with the bonus-action cost-reduction effect.

feats.name: Tough
feats.description:
You have the blood of heroes flowing through your veins. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You must now fail 5 Death saving throws before dying.
• Whenever you take the Dodge action in combat, you can spend one Hit Die to heal yourself. Roll the die, add your Constitution modifier, and regain a number of hit points equal to the total (minimum of one).
• If you would roll a natural 20 on a Death saving throw, you instead regain a number of hit points equal to your level.

feats.name: Acrobat
feats.description:
You become nimbler, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Acrobatics skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• You reduce falling damage by half.
• Once per turn, when standing from prone from a hostile creature's effect, you do not spend movement and you may make an unarmed strike or melee weapon attack against a creature within range as part of your movement used to stand from prone.
• As a bonus action, you can make a DC 15 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check. If you succeed, difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement until the end of your next turn.

actions.name: Nimble Sprint
actions.description: Make a DC 15 Dexterity (Acrobatics) check. On a success, difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement until the end of your next turn.

feats.name: Actor
feats.description:
Skilled at mimicry and dramatics, you gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Performance skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• When your Charisma (Performance) check is 5 or higher than a non-hostile creature's Passive Insight, they gain 1 rank of Charm towards you, until the end of this conversation. They can only gain 2 ranks of charm towards you this way.
• While performing, you can try to distract one humanoid you can see who can see and hear you. Make a Charisma (Performance) check contested by the humanoid's Wisdom (Insight) check. If your check succeeds, you grab the humanoid's attention enough that it makes Wisdom (Perception) and Intelligence (Investigation) checks with disadvantage until you stop performing.

feats.name: Alchemist
feats.description:
You have studied the secrets of alchemy and are an expert in its practice, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency with the Alchemist Kit. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• As an action, you can identify any one poison or consumable liquid within 5 feet of you, as if you had tasted it. You must see it for this benefit to work.
• Over the course of any short rest, you can create one set of Assassins Blood. You must have an Alchemist Kit with at least one charge with you when you do.
• Over the course of any short rest, you can temporarily improve the potency of one poison, chakra or blood pill. To use this benefit, you must spend the charge of the Alchemist kit. When you do, you enhance the following:
  ◦ Potency of a Poison — Increase its save DC by +2 and damage by one additional damage die.
  ◦ Potency of a Chakra Pill — Increase the chakra gained by 1 die.
  ◦ Potency of a Blood Pill — Increase the hit points gained by 2 dice.

actions.name: Identify Substance
actions.description: Identify one poison or consumable liquid within 5 feet (must see it) as if you had tasted it.

feats.name: Animal Handler
feats.description:
You master the techniques needed to train and handle animals. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Animal Handling skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• By spending 1 week of downtime with a normal animal, you can make a DC 20 Wisdom (Animal Handling) check. On a success, you bond with the animal. It becomes permanently helpful to you unless you do something egregious to break your bond. Bonding with a new animal breaks any previous bonds. New animals do not know any of your previous bonded animal's jutsu, if any.
• By spending 1 or more weeks of downtime with a bonded animal, you can teach it one D-Rank or higher Taijutsu that you know. Downtime cost: D-Rank: 1 week, C-Rank: 5 weeks, B-Rank: 10 weeks, A-Rank: 20 weeks, S-Rank: 50 weeks. The animal uses its bite and claws in place of punches or kicks. A creature can only learn 3 Taijutsu this way.
• You can use a bonus action on your turn to command your bonded animal within 60 feet of you that can hear you.

actions.name: Command Bonded Animal
actions.description: Command your bonded animal within 60 feet of you that can hear you.

feats.name: Brawny
feats.description:
You've become stronger, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase your Strength score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Athletics skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• Increase your maximum bulk by +10.
• Once per rest, you may make a Strength-based ability or skill check, attack roll or saving throw at advantage.
• Thrown weapons that use Strength double both range increments.

feats.name: Burglar
feats.description:
You pride yourself on your quickness and your close study of certain clandestine activities. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency with a Security Kit or Sleight of Hand (pick one). If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• Creatures who are further than 30 feet from you while you are using your Security Kit or making a Sleight of Hand check to pick a lock or steal something have a -5 penalty on Perception or Insight checks to see what you are doing.
• Locks and Security devices are always counted as 1 Rank lower for you.
• As a bonus action, you can attempt to pickpocket or pick a lock.

actions.name: Pickpocket / Pick Lock
actions.description: Attempt to pickpocket or pick a lock as a bonus action.

feats.name: Charming Persona
feats.description:
You've mastered the art of charming those around you, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Persuasion skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• You are skilled in gathering information through conversation, without asking a single question. By spending 10 minutes gathering information in a new place, you become aware of all current rumors and potentially important people you should be on the lookout for.
• If you spend 1 minute talking to someone, you can make a Charisma (Persuasion) check contested by a creature's passive Insight. If you or your companions are in combat with the creature, you automatically fail. On a success, the target gains 2 ranks of Charmed for you as long as it remains within 60 feet of you for the next 10 minutes.

feats.name: Chakra Intensity
feats.description:
You have an immense control over your chakra, in ways that others aspire to. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Chakra Control skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• You release an aura of chakra indicative of your combat ability. When making a Charisma (Intimidation) check, you may instead roll your Constitution (Chakra Control) bonus instead.
• You can suppress your own chakra signature in a far more efficient way. You can make the Suppress Chakra activity as an Action, instead of requiring 1 minute.

actions.name: Suppress Chakra (Quick)
actions.description: Make the Suppress Chakra skill action as an Action instead of the normal 1-minute cost.

feats.name: Chef
feats.description:
Time and effort spent mastering the culinary arts has paid off. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency with a Cooking Kit or Survival (pick one). If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• Over the course of a short rest, you can cook special food with a cooking kit. This special food is of your description and creatures consume this food during the short rest. They regain additional hit points equal to your Survival bonus and, for the next 8 hours or until your next rest, they temporarily increase their maximum hit points by 10. You can cook special food in this way twice per long rest.
• Over the course of a long rest, you can cook a special full-course meal for your team with a cooking kit. This special meal takes 1 hour to consume and spoils at the end of your long rest. Creatures who partake in the meal are cured of all diseases and poisons and make their next 2 Constitution saves at advantage. This benefit lasts for 24 hours.

feats.name: Chemist
feats.description:
You have studied the secrets of chemistry and are an expert in its practice, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency with the Alchemist Kit or Investigation (pick one). If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• When you would make an Intelligence (Investigation) check, and have at least one charge of Alchemist Kit to collect DNA, evidence or any remains from a location, you reduce the DC set by the DM by -2.
• Over the course of a short rest, you can develop different chemical compounds, using a Forensics Kit, which you then use to create one of the following of base quality: Chili Pepper Bomb, Smoke Bomb, Military Ration Pill or Chakra Pill.
• Over the course of a long rest, you can develop a special chemical with an Alchemist Kit called White water. White water has 2 charges and expires after 8 hours. If a creature drinks this White water, they gain one of the following benefits for the next hour: 60ft Chakra Sight, 60ft Darkvision, 20ft Tremor Sense or 10ft Blindsight.

feats.name: Crafter
feats.description:
You have a knack for crafting; you work with greater efficiency and produce goods of higher quality. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Strength, Intelligence or Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in Crafting and either Weaponsmiths or Armorsmith Kit (pick one). If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• When you craft items using a kit you have proficiency in, the item is worth double the market value.
• When you craft seals using either the Armorsmith or Weaponsmith kit you no longer need a Sealsmith forge.
• Reduce the Enhancement seal crafting DC by -2 across all item ranks.

feats.name: Elite Agility
feats.description:
You have the reflexes of one who can see things before they happen, granting the following benefits:
• You gain a 1d4 bonus to all Dexterity saving throws you make.
• Choose two Dexterity skills which you already have proficiency in. When you make a skill check using these skills, you treat any roll of 9 or lower on the d20 as a 10.
• You gain Mastery in one Dexterity skill of your choice which you already have proficiency in.
• Increase your initiative by +5 and your movement speed by +10.
• If a creature would have disadvantage on an attack against you, you can choose to make them roll an additional d20, using the lowest roll. Once you use this ability twice, you must complete a rest before using it again.

feats.name: Elite Awareness
feats.description:
You have the wisdom associated with the most renowned masters of Ninshou, which grants you a level of awareness most others don't have. You gain the following benefits:
• You gain a 1d4 bonus to all Wisdom saving throws you make.
• You gain Mastery in one Wisdom skill which you already have proficiency in.
• You gain a +5 bonus to your passive Perception and Insight.
• You can use your action to try and gain an exalted perception into your current situation. So long as you are not blind and/or deaf, all illusions, Genjutsu with the Visual and/or Auditory keyword, and effects designed to deceive you, within 60 feet of you, have their DCs reduced by 5 for the next minute, or until the start of your next turn if in combat.

actions.name: Exalted Perception
actions.description: So long as you are not blind/deaf, all illusions, Genjutsu with the Visual or Auditory keyword, and effects designed to deceive you within 60 feet have their DCs reduced by 5 for the next minute (or until the start of your next turn in combat).

feats.name: Elite Intellect
feats.description:
You have the cunning of the most prolific scholars, granting the following benefits:
• You gain a 1d4 bonus to all Intelligence saving throws you make.
• You gain Mastery in one Intelligence skill which you already have proficiency in.
• Intelligence ability and skill checks you make ignore any penalty inflicted by a creature or effect.
• You reduce the Downtime cost required to complete any training to the following:
  ◦ 1 Week: New Tool or Vehicle Proficiency.
  ◦ 3 Weeks: New Weapon or Language Proficiency.
  ◦ 5 Weeks: New Armor Proficiency.
  ◦ 8 Weeks: New Skill Proficiency.
  ◦ 16 Weeks: New Feat.

feats.name: Elite Presence
feats.description:
You have the presence of the most affluent of leaders, granting the following benefits:
• You gain a 1d4 bonus to all Charisma saving throws you make.
• You gain Mastery in one Charisma skill which you already have proficiency in.
• Allied creatures within 20 feet of you cannot gain ranks of Fear or Charm. Creatures who have such conditions who speak to you for at least 1 minute immediately lose such conditions and become immune to gaining them from the creature that inflicted it upon them for the next 24 hours.
• As an action, you can attempt to impose your Elite Presence upon another creature. So long as the creature you are interacting with is of equal or lower level than you, are not hostile, and can hear and see you, each minute they are interacting with you they gain a -2 penalty to all Wisdom and Charisma skill checks made against you. These penalties immediately end if they become hostile to you in any way.

actions.name: Impose Presence
actions.description: Attempt to impose your Elite Presence on a non-hostile creature of equal or lower level. Each minute they interact with you, they gain a -2 penalty to all Wisdom and Charisma skill checks made against you. Ends if they become hostile.

feats.name: Elite Resilience
feats.description:
You have the fortitude often attributed to gods, granting the following benefits:
• You gain a 1d4 bonus to all Constitution saving throws you make.
• You gain Mastery in one Constitution skill which you already have proficiency in.
• You cannot be Envenomed or succumb to disease as a result of non-jutsu-based methods.
• Restoration effects, such as Healing Jutsu or Military Ration pills, that would restore hit points to you can't restore an amount less than half your level + your Constitution modifier. If this amount would exceed the maximum amount of hit points that effect could restore, you instead take that effect's maximum.
• You can add your Constitution modifier to death saving throws you make.

feats.name: Elite Strength
feats.description:
You have the strength that legends tell of, granting the following benefits:
• You gain a 1d4 bonus to all Strength saving throws you make.
• You gain Mastery in one Strength skill which you already have proficiency in.
• You ignore the two-handed property of weapons with which you are proficient.
• You can attempt to use your Elite Strength to complete normally impossible acts of strength. Choose one of the following acts of superhuman Strength to complete. Once you use one of these features, you must complete a rest before you can do so again.
  ◦ Hold the Line — As an action, you ready yourself for anything until the end of your next turn. When a creature would make an attack against you, force you to make a saving throw, or use a feature targeting you, you immediately act at no further action cost. Make a Strength ability check, adding half your character level, vs the triggering creature's attack roll or Save DC. On a success, you dispel the attack, jutsu or effect. Triggers a number of times equal to your Strength modifier before the beginning of your next turn.
  ◦ Herculean — As a bonus action, for the next minute you can move anything you can put your hands on. You can Grapple, Shove, and Trip Gargantuan or smaller creatures as if they were your size.
  ◦ Unfallen — As a reaction to a structure or construct falling or collapsing within 5ft of you, you can exert your strength, preventing it from falling for the next minute. Regardless of how many hit points a structure has left, while you are holding it, it cannot collapse in on itself.

actions.name: Hold the Line
actions.description: Ready yourself until the end of your next turn. When a creature attacks you, forces a save, or uses a feature targeting you, immediately make a Strength ability check (+ half your character level) vs their attack/Save DC. On success, dispel the attack/jutsu/effect. Triggers up to your Strength modifier times before your next turn. Once per rest combined with the other Elite Strength acts.

actions.name: Herculean
actions.description: For 1 minute, you can move anything you can put your hands on. Grapple/Shove/Trip Gargantuan or smaller creatures as if they were your size. Once per rest combined with the other Elite Strength acts.

actions.name: Unfallen
actions.description: When a structure or construct falls or collapses within 5 ft of you, prevent it from falling for the next minute. Once per rest combined with the other Elite Strength acts.

feats.name: Empathic
feats.description:
You possess keen insight into how other people think and feel. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Insight skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• You can spend a bonus action to attempt an Insight check.
• If you spend more than 10 minutes talking to a non-hostile creature, you gain insight into their mannerisms allowing you to tell their emotional state and if they are lying for the remainder of the conversation. This grants insight into their emotional state and surface lies that require minor context. This does not reveal deeper lies that require significantly more context or evidence to prove.
• You can use your action to try to get uncanny insight about one humanoid you can see within 30 feet of you. Make a Wisdom (Insight) check contested by the target's Passive Deception. On a success, you become aware if any chakra-based illusions are affecting them or if they are currently experiencing an unbalanced mental state. This informs you if they are afraid of, enamored by, in love with or even loathe something or someone. You gain a clue as to who or what that thing or person is.

actions.name: Quick Insight
actions.description: Attempt an Insight check as a bonus action.

actions.name: Uncanny Insight
actions.description: Choose one humanoid within 30 feet you can see. Wisdom (Insight) check contested by their Passive Deception. On a success, learn if chakra-based illusions affect them or if they're in an unbalanced mental state, with a clue about its source.

feats.name: Feigned Confidence
feats.description:
You've spent years pretending you know what you're doing, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in Deception. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• When you would make a Charisma (Persuasion) check, you can choose to instead use your Charisma (Deception), as you weave white lies into your conversation or diplomatic statements in an attempt to gain favor with the other conversation participants. These white lies are harmless and cannot create negative reactions from other conversation participants.
• Select one skill you are not proficient in, excluding Athletics, Acrobatics or Chakra Control (you can change this skill when you complete a long rest). When you would make a check using the chosen skill you instead use your Deception bonus in place of that skill's bonus. If you succeed, you believed in yourself so much that you complete the task haphazardly. The completed task may pass as genuine work for a moment, but upon further inspection against a DC equal to your Deception bonus, others realize that it was potentially dumb luck or you did it in a very unprofessional way. (Ex: You use this feat to use Deception in place of Sleight of Hand to pick a lock. If you succeed, you may have picked the lock, but now the door or lock itself is also broken or there is evidence of tampering.)

feats.name: Genius Toolist
feats.description:
You have spent prolonged use with a toolkit, making you a genius when using it. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency with any one Toolkit. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• Whenever you would use the Toolkit to make a skill check, you can use your Intelligence in place of any other ability modifier.
• As an action, when viewing a construct, you may make an Intelligence ability check vs DC (5 + target's level, if any). On a success, you become aware of any vulnerabilities it may have (if any).
• If you are viewing a structure for at least 10 minutes per building size category, you can make an Intelligence ability check vs DC (8 + the size category of the structure: Small Building 3, Medium Building 5, Large Building 7, Huge Building 9, Gargantuan Building 11). On a success, you become aware of one or two secret passageway entrances or exits.

Building Sizes in relation to Genius Toolist:
While there are no direct rules for determining a building's size outside of its given dimensions, this feat assumes a DM has an approximation for the size of a structure. Based on the size categories provided here are examples of a given building within a given size category:
• Small Building: Average home, cottage, small office or shop, huts, slum buildings. Encompasses most living quarters for all Modest and below lifestyles.
• Medium Building: Wealthy home, hotel or inn, higher-end shops, forges, training facilities. Encompasses most living quarters for Comfortable lifestyles.
• Large Buildings: Mansions, compounds, Kage buildings/offices, military installations. Encompasses most living quarters for Wealthy lifestyles.
• Huge Buildings: Castles, palaces, daimyo estates. Encompasses most living quarters for unprecedented lifestyles.
• Gargantuan Buildings: Buildings the size of small towns, castle cities, mountain fortresses.

actions.name: Analyze Construct
actions.description: Make an Intelligence ability check vs DC (5 + target's level) on a construct you can see. On success, learn its vulnerabilities (if any).

feats.name: Perceptive
feats.description:
You hone your senses until they become razor sharp. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Perception skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• Being in a lightly obscured area doesn't impose disadvantage on your Wisdom (Perception) checks if you can both see and hear.
• You have a +5 bonus to your passive Wisdom (Perception) and passive Intelligence (Investigation) scores.
• If you can see a creature's mouth while it is speaking a dialect you understand, you can interpret what it's saying by reading its lips.

feats.name: Performer
feats.description:
Your years of training with your musical instrument have paid off. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Performance skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• When performing for at least one minute with your most practiced musical instrument, you gain advantage on a Performance skill check.
• Once per long rest, you can perform for your team with your most practiced musical instrument and inspire your allies. Your inspired allies gain temporary hit points equal to your proficiency bonus + Charisma ability score.

feats.name: Field Medic
feats.description:
You are an able medic, allowing you to mend wounds quickly and get your allies back in the fight. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in Medicine. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• You gain proficiency in Medicine Kits. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• When you would beat the stabilization DC by 10 or more, the target creature also regains 1 hit point becoming conscious and you remove 1 death saving throw. You can remove a death saving throw from a creature in this way twice per Full Rest, per creature.
• As a bonus action, you can spend a use of your Medicine Kit. If you do, you enhance the next Blood pill that you or another creature consumes. When you do, they instead gain the maximum benefit of consuming one of these pills and do not need to roll.

actions.name: Enhance Blood Pill
actions.description: Spend a use of your Medicine Kit to enhance the next Blood pill consumed by you or another creature — they gain the maximum benefit and don't roll.

feats.name: Historian
feats.description:
Your study of history rewards you with the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the History skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• Twice per long rest, when you take the Help action to aid another creature's check, you can make a DC 15 Intelligence (History) check. On a success, that creature's check gains a bonus equal to your Intelligence modifier, as you share pertinent advice and historical examples. You regain one use of this feature on a short rest.
• As an action on your turn or as a reaction to seeing a creature for the first time, select one creature you can see. Make an Intelligence (History) check vs DC (8 + creature's level). On a success, you become aware of this creature's primary jutsu type or method of combat as you recall reading or researching about them. You can only use this feature on a creature once per long rest. On a failure, you cannot immediately recall anything about them.

actions.name: Recall Combatant Lore
actions.description: On your turn (Action) or as a Reaction to seeing a creature for the first time, make an Intelligence (History) check vs DC (8 + creature's level). On a success, learn the creature's primary jutsu type or method of combat. Once per long rest per creature.

feats.name: Herbalist
feats.description:
You are adept at harnessing the useful properties of herbs and other plants. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in Nature. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• You can apply natural cures and medicines to heal your allies. So long as you are near a variety of plants and natural foliage you can make an Intelligence (Nature) check in place of a Medicine check.
• If you are in wilderness, you can treat the surrounding natural foliage as if you have a Medicine kit with 3 charges.
• Over the course of a short rest, you can make an Intelligence (Nature) check to search for special plants which can be used to create a special salve which you or another creature can consume or apply to another willing creature as an action. The DC to find the plants needed to create the salve is listed below. On a success, pick one of the salves you want to make that has a DC lower than or equal to your check's result. You can only make one Salve per rest. These Salves retain their potency for up to 24 hours. You can only have two salves at any given time. If you ever gain a third salve, one of the previous salves loses potency and becomes inert. You can only find special plants for these salves in any given location within 10 miles twice per month.

Herbalist Salves:
• DC 10 — Bitter Salve: End the Envenomed condition and recover 2d10 + 4 hit points.
• DC 12 — Sweet Salve: Regain the ability to mold chakra and recover 2d6 + 4 chakra points.
• DC 15 — Gelatinous Salve: Select one damage type. You reduce all damage inflicted by that damage type by 5 for 1 minute.
• DC 17 — Sour Salve: End the Charmed or Feared condition and recover 2d12 + 12 hit points.
• DC 20 — Tangy Salve: End the Stunned condition and recover 2d8 + 12 chakra points.
• DC 22 — Salty Salve: Regain 1d4 + 1 hit points at the end of each of your turns for 1 minute.
• DC 25 — Rock Salve: Select two damage types. You gain resistance to those damage types.
• DC 27 — Taffy Salve: Your AC becomes 15 + your proficiency bonus for 1 minute if it is lower than the result. Your AC cannot be reduced below this value for the duration.
• DC 30 — Omni Salve: Select two of any previous salves and combine their effects.

actions.name: Apply Salve
actions.description: Consume or apply a prepared salve to a willing creature. See the Herbalist Salves list in the description for effects per salve.

feats.name: Investigator
feats.description:
You have an eye for detail and can pick out the smallest clues. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in Investigation or the Forensics Kit (pick one). If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• You can take the Search action or make an Intelligence (Investigation) check as a bonus action.
• When you make an Intelligence (Investigation) check, if your result is 5 or higher than the DC, you gain additional information or clues into the given mystery or situation.
• By spending 10 minutes studying an area the size of a small room, you can begin to see echoes of the last 24 hours in the given area. This echo plays in your mind as if watching a movie backwards and all the actors are featureless humanoid figures which you cannot attribute a name or face to. This scene plays out in its entirety, and you can rewind, slow down, or speed up this echo at will. You cannot replay an echo if 24 hours has passed. While viewing this echo, you can make Intelligence (Investigation) checks of varying difficulty set by the DM to gather information from the echo based on what's happening.

actions.name: Search / Investigate
actions.description: Take the Search action or make an Intelligence (Investigation) check as a bonus action.

actions.name: Echo Reading
actions.description: Spend 10 minutes studying an area the size of a small room. See echoes of the last 24 hours in the area as featureless humanoid figures; rewind, slow, or speed up at will, and make Investigation checks (DM-set DCs) to gather information.

feats.name: Master of Disguise
feats.description:
You have honed your ability to shape your personality and visage into that of others with a surprising level of detail. You gain the following benefits:
• You gain proficiency with the Disguise Kit.
• You gain proficiency in Performance. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• You can make Charisma (Performance) checks in place of Deception checks.
• When you are mimicking a target, you have advantage on Performance checks made to impersonate that target.
• Reduce the time it takes to make a disguise when using a Disguise Kit by a factor of up to 10. Plain disguises now only take 1 minute. Elaborate disguises now only take 10 minutes, and exquisite disguises now only take 1 hour.

feats.name: Poisoner
feats.description:
You have studied the secrets of poisons and toxins, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency with the Poisoner's Kit. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• Reduce the DC to create poisons by an amount equal to your Ninjutsu ability modifier.
• You can apply a poison to a weapon as a part of the same action used to attack with the weapon, once per turn.
• During a short rest, you can infuse your chakra into a single dose of poison. To use this benefit, you must have a Poisoner's Kit and a poison of any type. You can only have one infused poison this way. This infused poison can use either its listed Save DC or your Ninjutsu Save DC (you decide). Additionally, if it deals damage, it deals additional damage equal to an amount of damage dice equal to half of your proficiency bonus.

actions.name: Apply Poison
actions.description: Apply a poison to a weapon as part of the same action used to attack with that weapon. Once per turn.

feats.name: Practiced Adept
feats.description:
You have acquired skills over your career, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase an ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in any combination of three skills or tools of your choice.
• You can take this feat multiple times.

feats.name: Practiced Expert
feats.description:
You have honed your proficiency with particular skills or tools, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase any ability score of your choice by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain +1 ranks of Mastery in any two skills or toolkits you are proficient in. You cannot select the same skill/toolkit twice.
• You can take this feat multiple times.

feats.name: Quick Fingered
feats.description:
Your nimble fingers and agility let you perform sleight of hand. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Sleight of Hand skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• You can perform a Sleight of Hand check as a bonus action.
• Your skills in thievery are so great that you can even pickpocket or plant something up to 30 feet away.
• As a reaction to a creature making a weapon attack targeting you, you can make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check vs the target's attack roll. On a success, you disarm them so quickly that they are unaware you have disarmed them. Their attack becomes an unarmed attack. They don't become aware that they have been disarmed until after the attack has concluded.

actions.name: Sleight of Hand (Quick)
actions.description: Perform a Sleight of Hand check as a bonus action.

actions.name: Reactive Disarm
actions.description: When a creature makes a weapon attack targeting you, make a Dexterity (Sleight of Hand) check vs their attack roll. On success, silently disarm them — their attack becomes unarmed and they don't realize until after.

feats.name: Stealthy
feats.description:
You know how best to hide. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Stealth skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• You can perform the Hide action as a bonus action.
• If you are hidden, you gain a +20 bonus to your movement speed when sneaking.
• Creatures have a -5 penalty to Wisdom (Perception) checks made to search for you, while you are hidden from them.

actions.name: Hide (Quick)
actions.description: Take the Hide action as a bonus action.

feats.name: Menacing
feats.description:
You become fearsome to others, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase your Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Intimidation skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• You can attempt the Demoralize skill action as a bonus action.
• You can attempt the Demoralize skill action against the same creature up to three times before they become accustomed to your intimidating presence.

actions.name: Demoralize (Quick)
actions.description: Attempt the Demoralize skill action as a bonus action.

feats.name: Ninshou Research
feats.description:
You have begun to deep dive into the lore, mechanics and purpose of Ninjutsu. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Ninshou skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• When you would make an Identify Ninjutsu check, you reduce the DC by 5.
• If you would attempt a Read the Enemy check on a creature casting a Ninjutsu, you no longer need to see a creature using handsigns to attempt this check, and you increase the AC bonus you would gain to +3.
• You reduce the downtime needed to learn Ninjutsu using the self-taught method by an amount equal to the rank of the jutsu (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).

feats.name: Martial Arts Research
feats.description:
You have begun to research the history of martial arts and how they have changed over the generations. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence or Strength by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Martial Arts skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• When you would make an Identify Taijutsu/Bukijutsu check, you reduce the DC by 5.
• If you would attempt a Read the Enemy check on a creature casting a Taijutsu or Bukijutsu, you gain 5 damage reduction to the Taijutsu or Bukijutsu cast.
• Select one between Taijutsu or Bukijutsu. You reduce the downtime needed to learn the chosen type using the self-taught method by an amount equal to the rank of the jutsu (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).

feats.name: Illusionist Research
feats.description:
You have begun to find some truth in the world of illusions and Genjutsu, finding its origins to be darker and more sinister than you once thought. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain proficiency in the Illusions skill. If you are already proficient, you instead gain Mastery.
• When you would make an Identify Genjutsu check, you reduce the DC by 5.
• If you would attempt a Read the Enemy check on a creature casting a Genjutsu, you no longer need to see a creature using handsigns to attempt this check, and you gain a +2 bonus to saving throws made against this Genjutsu's casting until the beginning of your next turn.
• You reduce the downtime needed to learn Genjutsu using the self-taught method by an amount equal to the rank of the jutsu (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).

feats.name: Chakra Construction
feats.description:
You've learned to efficiently create constructs of chakra, maximizing their potential for less. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence or Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• When you are concentrating on a jutsu with the Construct keyword, the first time you would make a concentration check as the result of damage per round, you automatically succeed the check.
• When you cast a jutsu with the Construct keyword, you may apply two of the following benefits. You cannot select the same benefit more than once:
  ◦ Damage rolls from these constructs pierce any passive DR a target possesses.
  ◦ These constructs increase their attack rolls by +1, and impose a -1 penalty to saving throws they impose.
  ◦ These constructs gain an amount of DR against all damage equal to your jutsu casting modifier.
  ◦ These constructs increase their AC by half your jutsu casting modifier.

feats.name: Chakra Guidance
feats.description:
You've learned to utilize your gift with chakra in a specific unique way. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Wisdom or Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• Choose a skill in which you are proficient. When you make a skill check with the chosen skill, you can use your Wisdom or Charisma modifier in place of its original ability score.
• Jutsu you encounter are far easier to deal with due to your unique quirks with chakra. When you would attempt to interrupt or dispel a jutsu another creature cast or are currently concentrating on, you gain a 1d4 bonus towards any checks you make towards interrupting or dispelling their jutsu.
• Jutsu you cast are far more difficult to recognize, and therefore more difficult to plan against. When a creature would attempt to interrupt or dispel a jutsu you cast or are concentrating on, they take a 1d4 penalty to any checks made to do either of those.

feats.name: Chakra Pressure
feats.description:
You have a very powerful chakra aura. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• Select between: Ninjutsu, Genjutsu, Taijutsu or Bukijutsu. Jutsu you use that have one of the aforementioned keywords, that require a creature make a saving throw, cannot add jutsu-based bonuses to their save unless the jutsu granting the bonus is of a rank higher than the jutsu you originally cast.
• Your chakra is heavy and carries a weight unlike any other. Jutsu you cast with the chakra molding (CM) component, that summons a creature, construct or object with hit points or temporary hit points — that creature, construct or object gains a number of additional hit points equal to twice your Constitution modifier (min 2).
• Your chakra-based constructs are more difficult for enemies to disrupt or dispel. When a creature would attempt to interrupt or dispel a jutsu or feature you cast that would summon a creature, construct or object with hit points or temporary hit points, increase their DC to do so by +2.

feats.name: Endurance, Latent
feats.description:
You have a great deal of chakra within you ready to come out. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• When you roll a Chakra Die to regain chakra points, you treat all 1's as 2's.
• Your chakra point maximum increases by an amount equal to your level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a level thereafter, your chakra points maximum increases by an additional 1 chakra point.
• As a full turn action, you can draw on the well of chakra within yourself. You can spend up to 4 chakra die. When you do, roll all chakra die spent, gaining chakra points equal to the result. You then gain 1 rank of Exhaustion for the next hour.

actions.name: Draw Chakra Reserves
actions.description: Spend up to 4 chakra dice and roll them, gaining chakra points equal to the result. You then gain 1 rank of Exhaustion for the next hour.

feats.name: Endurance, Realized
feats.description:
You have a much deeper pool of chakra reserves than previously thought. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain resistance to Chakra damage.
• Your chakra point maximum increases by an amount equal to your level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a level thereafter, your chakra points maximum increases by an additional 1 chakra point.
• Your chakra point maximum cannot be reduced by hostile effects.

feats.name: Improved Chakra Efficiency
feats.description:
You have gained greater control over your chakra, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You may select 2 jutsu you currently have. That jutsu's cost is reduced by an amount equal to its rank. You can change this choice when you would complete a long rest. (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5)
• You may select one class feature you currently have that costs chakra. You may use this feature at 0 cost, twice per long rest. If the feature selected can have its cost increased for a larger benefit, it cannot be selected as an option for this feat. If a feature has sub-features and/or multiple costs, pick one to apply this feat to.
• If you do not have a class feature that costs chakra, you instead gain 1 additional use of a class feature that would normally refresh when you complete a short or long rest. You cannot switch the feature that this feat benefits.

feats.name: Medical Release
feats.description:
You learn how to mold chakra in ways befitting of a bonafide Medical-Nin. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You are now able to add jutsu with the Medical keyword.
• You learn one jutsu with the Medical keyword.
• Over the course of a long rest, you can remove any one condition or disease of C-Rank or lower currently affecting yourself or a single ally who you can reach for the duration of the long rest. At the end of the rest, they are healed of the disease or condition, gain the benefits of a long rest, and gain resistance to the disease or condition until their next rest.

feats.name: Nature Release
feats.description:
You learn how to mold chakra into one of the 5 elements. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You select one of the 5 following Nature Releases: Fire, Water, Lightning, Wind, Earth. You are now able to add jutsu with the corresponding keyword to your jutsu list.
• You learn one jutsu with the corresponding Nature Release that you selected, that you qualify for.
• You can take this feat more than once, each time selecting a different nature release.

feats.name: Streamlined Chakra Efficiency
feats.description:
Your chakra network is unlike most others. You can get twice the results from your network in half the effort, granting you the following benefits:
• Increase your Constitution score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• Select one category of jutsu between Ninjutsu, Genjutsu, Taijutsu or Bukijutsu. The cost to maintain concentration of a jutsu of the chosen type is reduced by 1, to a minimum of 1.
• When you would successfully complete a short rest and spend 3 or more chakra die, you regain 1 chakra die at the conclusion of calculating how much chakra you regain.
• You gain DR (damage reduction) vs Chakra damage equal to your proficiency bonus.
• Hostile effects that would increase the base cost of jutsu you cast have their effects halved. This does not count for the Sealed condition. (Ex: If a hostile effect increases the base cost of a jutsu you cast by 4, it instead only increases it by 2.)

feats.name: Seal Resistant
feats.description:
Your chakra is resilient to the effects of most sealing attempts. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Constitution or Charisma score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You gain a +2 bonus to Charisma saving throws vs jutsu with the Fuinjutsu keyword.
• You make checks to remove the Sealed condition at advantage.
• If a jutsu, effect, feature or trait would attempt to remove your ability to mold chakra without a saving throw, you instead make a Constitution saving throw vs the jutsu type's save DC. If the effect was not caused by a jutsu, you instead make a DC 20 Constitution saving throw, retaining your ability to mold chakra on a successful save.

feats.name: Clone Specialization
feats.prerequisite: Any Ninjutsu with the Clone Keyword, Level 4+
feats.description:
You've become adept at ordering your clones to make a series of tactical maneuvers and actions. You gain the following benefits:
• When you summon no more than two clones, and are within 60 feet of terrain that could obscure them that you can see, you can make an Intelligence (Stealth) check vs the highest hostile creatures' passive perception. On a success, your clones are undetected.
• When you summon at least 3 clones, and a hostile creature you can see is within 30 feet of you, you can cause your summons to flank the target by summoning them in spaces adjacent to them. The target suffers a -1 penalty to their AC until the beginning of their next turn for each clone summoned within 5 feet of them (Max -3).
• As a bonus action, all clones within 60 feet of you move up to 30 feet, ending their movement as close as possible to a single hostile creature you can see within 60 feet. For every clone that ends its movement within 15 feet of them, you increase the next melee attack's damage you deal by 1d8.
• As a bonus action, by commanding your clones to Rush and Raid, you select up to three hostile creatures you can see within 60 feet of you. All clones are forced to move up to 60 feet, ending their movement as close as possible to the target. For every clone that ends its movement within 15 feet of the hostile creature, the next jutsu the target casts that would increase its AC, gain temporary hit points, or reduce damage suffers a penalty based on the following, until the end of their next turn:
  ◦ Bonus AC: Reduce AC bonus by -1 for every 2 clones within range.
  ◦ Temporary Hit Points: Reduce temporary hit points gained by 1d4 for every clone within range.
  ◦ Damage Reduction: Reduce DR benefit by -1 for every clone within range.

actions.name: Clone Strike Setup
actions.description: All clones within 60 feet move up to 30 feet, ending as close as possible to a single hostile creature within 60 feet. For each clone that ends within 15 feet of them, your next melee attack's damage increases by 1d8.

actions.name: Rush and Raid
actions.description: Select up to three hostile creatures you can see within 60 feet. All clones move up to 60 feet, ending as close as possible to the targets. Per clone within 15 feet, the target's next defensive jutsu suffers penalties to bonus AC / temp HP / DR until the end of their next turn.

feats.name: Earth Release Expert
feats.description:
Your mastery with earth-natured chakra is so great, you emanate gold, silver, and platinum chakra each time you mold earth chakra to cast a Ninjutsu with the earth release keyword. When casting a Ninjutsu with the earth release keyword, you gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• Constructs and structures you summon that would intercept damage gain additional hit points equal to the rank of the jutsu cast (D-Rank: +4, C-Rank: +8, B-Rank: +12, A-Rank: +16, S-Rank: +20).
• Constructs, structures, and objects made as a result of jutsu cast with the earth release keyword are no longer vulnerable to Lightning damage.
• A jutsu cannot benefit from more than one "X" Release Expert feat at a time.

feats.name: Elemental Chakra Penetration
feats.description:
When you gain this feat, choose one of the following damage types: Earth, Wind, Fire, Cold, Lightning. Ninjutsu you cast ignore resistance to damage of the chosen type.
In addition, when you roll damage for a jutsu you cast that deals damage of that type, you can reroll 1's and 2's, keeping the second result.
You can select this feat multiple times. Each time you do so, you must choose a different damage type.

feats.name: Elemental Specialization
feats.description:
You have begun to specialize in a particular element of your choice. You gain the following benefits:
• Select one Nature Release keyword (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, Lightning). You may ignore Hand Seal (HS) requirement for Ninjutsu of C-Rank or lower with your chosen nature release.
• You deal additional damage with Ninjutsu with your chosen Nature Release keyword, equal to its rank (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).
• You reduce the chakra cost of Ninjutsu with your chosen Nature Release keyword by an amount equal to its rank (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).
• You can take this feat multiple times, each time selecting a different element.

feats.name: Empowered Ninjutsu
feats.description:
You learn to empower your Ninjutsu with more precise chakra, empowering its effects. You gain the following benefits:
• When you cast a Ninjutsu you know, you can spend 10 chakra. If you do, you double the jutsu's potential impact granting it one of the following effects:
  ◦ If your Ninjutsu requires an attack roll, when you roll damage dice, you treat all 1's and 2's as 3.
  ◦ If your Ninjutsu forces a creature to make a saving throw, if they would take a reaction in response to your Ninjutsu, they double the chakra cost of the jutsu used. If the reaction they took does not cost chakra, they instead must spend 10 chakra to take the reaction.
• Ninjutsu you cast cannot have its damage reduced by more than half as a result of jutsu, features or traits. Creatures with natural resistances or immunities still reduce damage as normal.
• When you would make a Constitution (Chakra Control) check to maintain concentration on a Ninjutsu you cast, you may add a bonus to the check equal to the highest ranked Ninjutsu you are concentrating on (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).

feats.name: Fire Release Expert
feats.description:
Your mastery with fire-natured chakra is so great, flames you conjure burn twice as hot, with half the effort. When casting a Ninjutsu with the fire release keyword, you gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• For every fire damage die you roll, you pierce through 1 of a creature's DR (damage reduction), up to a max of 10.
• When you would cast a Ninjutsu with the fire release keyword that creates a cone, sphere or line, you can increase the size of the area or length of the line by 10 feet.
• A jutsu cannot benefit from more than one "X" Release Expert feat at a time.

feats.name: Lightning Release Expert
feats.description:
Your mastery with lightning-natured chakra is so great, you occasionally spark with residual energy. You can power most mundane technology passively. The lightning that you generate is so potent that it can pierce through even the greatest of defenses. When casting a Ninjutsu with the lightning release keyword, you gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• Creatures cannot have advantage on saving throws vs Ninjutsu you cast with the lightning release keyword.
• Reduce the DC made to maintain concentration on Ninjutsu with the lightning release keyword by -2.
• A jutsu cannot benefit from more than one "X" Release Expert feat at a time.

feats.name: Medical Chakra Penetration
feats.description:
When you gain this feat, choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Necrotic or Poison. Ninjutsu you cast ignore resistance to damage of the chosen type.
In addition, when you roll damage for a jutsu you cast that deals damage of that type, you can reroll 1's and 2's, keeping the second result.
You can select this feat multiple times. Each time you do so, you must choose a different damage type.

feats.name: Medical Release Expert
feats.description:
Every spark of life energy, corrupted dark chakra, or poisonous surge you manifest has a small piece of yourself within them. You can command the powers of life and death. When casting a Ninjutsu with the medical keyword, you gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence or Wisdom score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• Acid, Poison or Necrotic damage you deal ignores resistance.
• When you would restore a creature's hit points, other than yourself, you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to the number of healing dice rolled.
• A jutsu cannot benefit from more than one "X" Release Expert feat at a time.

feats.name: Ninjutsu Archivist
feats.description:
Your focus in the heat of combat and mastery of handseals allow you to weave Ninjutsu with much greater efficiency. You gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• You learn an additional Ninjutsu that you qualify for. This does not count against your Jutsu Known.
• The next time you would hit 5th, 9th, or 13th level, you learn one additional Ninjutsu of 1 rank lower than your highest known jutsu rank.
• If you take this feat after you have passed the previously stated levels, you instead gain 1 additional D-Rank if past 5th level, 1 additional C-Rank if past 9th level, and 1 additional B-Rank if past 13th level.

feats.name: Ninshou Theorist
feats.description:
You study the Ninshou arts, gaining the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• Reduce the downtime required to Learn, Modify, and Create Ninjutsu by 1 week, to a minimum of 1 week.
• Over the course of a short rest, you attempt to theorize and bend the chakra flow of Ninjutsu you know to be more effective. Select a number of Ninjutsu you know equal to your Intelligence modifier (Max 4) and make an Intelligence (Ninshou) check for each jutsu. The DC for each check is based on the rank of the jutsu (D-Rank: 16, C-Rank: 17, B-Rank: 18, A-Rank: 19, S-Rank: 20). On a success, until your next rest, each selected jutsu is always under one of the following Efficient Moldings from the Ninjutsu Specialist class at no additional chakra cost (you may choose a different Efficient Molding for each jutsu):
  ◦ Careful Ninjutsu (Max of 3 creatures.)
  ◦ Distant Ninjutsu
  ◦ Piercing Ninjutsu
  ◦ Subtle Ninjutsu
  ◦ Tenacious Ninjutsu
  ◦ Widened Ninjutsu

feats.name: Non-Elemental Specialization
feats.description:
You have begun to specialize in some form of non-elemental Ninjutsu, be it Fuinjutsu or Medical Ninjutsu. You gain the following benefits:
• Select one: Fuinjutsu or Medical. You may ignore Hand Seal (HS) requirement for Ninjutsu with the chosen keyword of C-Rank or lower.
• You deal additional damage with Ninjutsu with the chosen keyword, equal to its rank (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).
• You reduce the chakra cost of Ninjutsu with the chosen keyword by an amount equal to its rank (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).

feats.name: Water Release Expert
feats.description:
You can feel every droplet of water and every molecule of chakra flowing within each drop. You can squeeze efficiency out of every drop. When casting a Ninjutsu with the water release keyword, you gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• When you are near a sufficient source of water that can be used to reduce the cost of the given jutsu cast, reduce the cost by 3, instead of 2.
• When a Ninjutsu you cast with the water release keyword deals damage to at least one creature, you can choose to leave behind a small source of water within 5 ft of one of the affected creature(s). The source of water can be used to activate effects that require a sufficient source of water no more than once, which then drains the water source.
• A jutsu cannot benefit from more than one "X" Release Expert feat at a time.

feats.name: Wind Release Expert
feats.description:
Your mastery with wind-natured chakra is so great that with but a thought, you can create the sharpest blade made exclusively from wind. When casting a Ninjutsu with the wind release keyword, you gain the following benefits:
• Increase your Intelligence score by 1, to a maximum of 20.
• When you would trigger a Swirl effect, increase the radius by +5 feet.
• Creatures cannot have advantage on saving throws vs Ninjutsu you cast with the wind release keyword.
• A jutsu cannot benefit from more than one "X" Release Expert feat at a time.

feats.name: Storing Seal Soul
feats.description:
